site.title: U.S. Trade Tracker
site.tagline: Tracking the 2025 US Trade negotiations following US tariff announcements.
site.projectBy: A project by the Takshashila Institution

posts.title: What are EU’s Digital barriers?
posts.excerpt: Explainer on EU’s Digital taxes and laws and their impact on US trade negotiations. Digital services and goods form a large portion of US-EU trade. Of the total EU-US trade in services, a striking 77.2% are digital including intellectual property, digital business services, cloud computing, among others. In terms of value, trade in services between […]

posts.title: Why were they tariffed?
posts.excerpt: Understanding the logic behind some of highest tariffed nations with some of the lowest trade volumes with the US. In 2025, US Tariff rates reach an historic high with an average rate of 17.7%. Although most countries became subjects to these tariffs, some major players such as the USMCA countries, EU countries, China, India, UK […]

posts.title: Why were they not tariffed?
posts.excerpt: Understanding the countries who escaped reciprocal tariffs. Belarus, Burkina Faso, Cuba, North Korea, Palau, Russia, Seychelles, Somalia and Vatican City made it to a short list of countries that were not a subject to Trump’s reciprocal tariffs on ‘Liberation Day’, April 2, 2025. Canada and Mexico were also excluded from these reciprocal tariffs but due […]

posts.title: What is Trump’s Tariff formula?
posts.excerpt: An explainer on the factors determining these tariff What are Tariffs and how are they determined? Tariffs are defined as taxes imposed by a government on goods and services imported from other countries. These are determined by several economic, social, and political factors including the classification of a good, and its country of origin. Domestic consideration […]

posts.title: What are SPS measures?
posts.excerpt: understanding a key negotiation issue of Japan and India SPS or Sanitary and phytosanitary measures are health and safety measures that are adopted by countries to ensure the safety in the quality of imports that come from other countries. These are aimed at preventing health hazard for humans, plants or animals from an external invasive […]

posts.title: US-Japan Trade Negotiations
posts.excerpt: US-Japan trade summary (2024) In 2024, US goods imports from Japan were ~$152.06 billion, ~4.5 % of total US goods imports Japan’s top export destination is the US, accounting for ~20 % of its total exports worth $141 billion in 2024. ~11.4 % of Japan’s imports in 2024 came from the US amounting to $84.7 billion. US exported $79.7 billion in goods […]

posts.title: US-UK Trade Negotiations
posts.excerpt: US-UK trade summary (2024) In 2024, US goods imports from the UK were $68.1 billion, representing ~2.2% of total US merchandise imports. ~21.7% of UK exports were destined for the US in 2023–24 ~13.2% of UK imports were sourced from the US during the same period. US goods exports to the UK were $79.9 b in 2024, […]

posts.title: US-Canada Trade Negotiations
posts.excerpt: US-Canada trade summary (2024) In 2024, US goods imports from Canada totaled approximately $412.7 billion, accounting for about 17 % of total US goods imports. Over 76.45% of Canadian exports are destined for the US, largely driven by integrated supply chains, especially in energy, manufacturing and automotive sectors totaled approximately $435.17 billion. Roughly 65 % of Canada’s […]

posts.title: US-EU Trade Negotiations
posts.excerpt: US-EU trade summary (2024) In 2024, US imported $606 billion in goods from the EU, representing ~19.7 % of total US goods imports (~$3.07 trillion). ~20.6 % of EU goods exports in 2024 (~$585 billion) were destined for the US. ~13.7 % of EU goods imports (~$367 billion) originated from the US in 2024. In 2024, US exported ~$370 billion in goods to […]

posts.title: What is EU’s Anti-Coercion Instrument
posts.excerpt: A brief explainer of EU’s trade ‘Bazooka’ The European Union’s Anti-Coercion Instrument (ACI) is a strategic trade defence tool designed to deter and respond to economic coercion by non-EU countries in 2023. Economic coercion refers to actions by a third country that intentionally pressure the EU or a Member State to change a policy decision, […]

posts.title: US-China Trade Negotiations
posts.excerpt: US-China trade summary (2024) In 2024, US goods imports from China reached ~$438.9 billion, comprising ~13.3 % of total US goods imports (~$3.3 trillion). ~14.7 % of China’s total exports in 2024 were destined for the US. US goods accounted for ~6.3 % of China’s total imports in 2024. US goods exports to China were $143.5 billion, representing ~7.0 % of US […]

posts.title: US-India Trade Negotiations
posts.excerpt: US-India trade summary (2024) In 2024, US goods imports from India were ~$87.4 billion, representing ~2.6 % of total US imports. US imports from India (goods + services) were ~$77.52 billion in 2024, making up ~ 17.73 % of India’s total exports. US exports to India of $41.8 billion represent ~6.04 % of India’s total imports US goods exports to India ~$41.8 b […]

pages.title: Understanding the Politics
pages.excerpt: A detailed take on the politics behind the numbers and the rhetoric Under Trump, negotiations have been less about data, policy and a calculated responses and more about politics, personal relationships and the associated unpredictability. This segment deals with all the key political tools, events and crisis that shaped these negotiations and deals with issues […]
